118	/** Options for a plugin entry that registers providers, tools, commands, or services. */
119	type DefinePluginEntryOptions = {
120	  id: string;
121	  name: string;
122	  description: string;
123	  kind?: OpenClawPluginDefinition["kind"];
124	  configSchema?: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema | (() => OpenClawPluginConfigSchema);
125	  register: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
126	};
127	
128	/** Normalized object shape that OpenClaw loads from a plugin entry module. */
129	type DefinedPluginEntry = {
130	  id: string;
131	  name: string;
132	  description: string;
133	  configSchema: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema;
134	  register: NonNullable<OpenClawPluginDefinition["register"]>;
135	} & Pick<OpenClawPluginDefinition, "kind">;
136	
137	/** Resolve either a concrete config schema or a lazy schema factory. */
138	function resolvePluginConfigSchema(
139	  configSchema: DefinePluginEntryOptions["configSchema"] = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
140	): OpenClawPluginConfigSchema {
141	  return typeof configSchema === "function" ? configSchema() : configSchema;
142	}
143	
144	/**
145	 * Canonical entry helper for non-channel plugins.
146	 *
147	 * Use this for provider, tool, command, service, memory, and context-engine
148	 * plugins. Channel plugins should use `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` from
149	 * `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` so they inherit the channel capability wiring.
150	 */
151	export function definePluginEntry({
152	  id,
153	  name,
154	  description,
155	  kind,
156	  configSchema = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
157	  register,
158	}: DefinePluginEntryOptions): DefinedPluginEntry {
159	  return {
160	    id,
161	    name,
162	    description,
163	    ...(kind ? { kind } : {}),
164	    configSchema: resolvePluginConfigSchema(configSchema),
165	    register,
166	  };
167	}

1	# Extensions Boundary
2	
3	This directory contains bundled plugins. Treat it as the same boundary that
4	third-party plugins see.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
14	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
15	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md`
16	  - `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
17	- Definition files:
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
22	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
23	  - `package.json`
24	
25	## Boundary Rules
26	
27	- Extension production code should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and its
28	  own local barrels such as `./api.ts` and `./runtime-api.ts`.
29	- Do not import core internals from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`,
30	  `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`, or another extension's `src/**`.
31	- Do not use relative imports that escape the current extension package root.
32	- Keep plugin metadata accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json` and the package
33	  `openclaw` block so discovery and setup work without executing plugin code.
34	- Treat files like `src/**`, `onboard.ts`, and other local helpers as private
35	  unless you intentionally promote them through `api.ts` and, if needed, a
36	  matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade.
37	- If core or core tests need a bundled plugin helper, export it from `api.ts`
38	  first instead of letting them deep-import extension internals.
39	
40	## Expanding The Boundary
41	
42	- If an extension needs a new seam, add a typed Plugin SDK subpath or additive
43	  export instead of reaching into core.
44	- Keep new plugin-facing seams backwards-compatible and versioned. Third-party
45	  plugins consume this surface.
46	- When intentionally expanding the contract, update the docs, exported subpath
47	  list, package exports, and API/contract checks in the same change.
48

1	import {
2	  AllowFromListSchema,
3	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
4	  DmPolicySchema,
5	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
6	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";
7	import { buildSecretInputSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input";
8	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
9	
10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);

1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
86	}).superRefine((value, ctx) => {
87	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
88	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
89	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});

1	# @openclaw/nostr
2	
3	Nostr DM channel plugin for OpenClaw using NIP-04 encrypted direct messages.
4	
5	## Overview
6	
7	This extension adds Nostr as a messaging channel to OpenClaw. It enables your bot to:
8	
9	- Receive encrypted DMs from Nostr users
10	- Send encrypted responses back
11	- Work with any NIP-04 compatible Nostr client (Damus, Amethyst, etc.)
12	
13	## Installation
14	
15	```bash
16	openclaw plugins install @openclaw/nostr
17	```
18	
19	## Quick Setup
20	
21	1. Generate a Nostr keypair (if you don't have one):
22	
23	   ```bash
24	   # Using nak CLI
25	   nak key generate
26	
27	   # Or use any Nostr key generator
28	   ```
29	
30	2. Add to your config:
31	
32	   ```json
33	   {
34	     "channels": {
35	       "nostr": {
36	         "privateKey": "${NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY}",
37	         "relays": ["wss://relay.damus.io", "wss://nos.lol"]
38	       }
39	     }
40	   }
41	   ```
42	
43	3. Set the environment variable:
44	
45	   ```bash
46	   export NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY="nsec1..."  # or hex format
47	   ```
48	
49	4. Restart the gateway
50	
51	## Configuration
52	
53	| Key          | Type     | Default                                     | Description                                                |
54	| ------------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
55	| `privateKey` | string   | required                                    | Bot's private key (nsec or hex format)                     |
56	| `relays`     | string[] | `["wss://relay.damus.io", "wss://nos.lol"]` | WebSocket relay URLs                                       |
57	| `dmPolicy`   | string   | `"pairing"`                                 | Access control: `pairing`, `allowlist`, `open`, `disabled` |
58	| `allowFrom`  | string[] | `[]`                                        | Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex)                       |
59	| `enabled`    | boolean  | `true`                                      | Enable/disable the channel                                 |
60	| `name`       | string   | -                                           | Display name for the account                               |
61	
62	## Access Control
63	
64	### DM Policies
65	
66	- **pairing** (default): Unknown senders receive a pairing code to request access
67	- **allowlist**: Only pubkeys in `allowFrom` can message the bot
68	- **open**: Anyone can message the bot (use with caution)
69	- **disabled**: DMs are disabled
70	
71	Inbound event signatures are verified before policy enforcement and NIP-04 decryption.
72	Unknown senders in `pairing` mode can receive a pairing reply, but their original DM body is not
73	processed unless approved.
74	
75	### Example: Allowlist Mode
76	
77	```json
78	{
79	  "channels": {
80	    "nostr": {
81	      "privateKey": "${NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY}",
82	      "dmPolicy": "allowlist",
83	      "allowFrom": ["npub1abc...", "0123456789abcdef..."]
84	    }
85	  }
86	}
87	```
88	
89	## Testing
90	
91	### Local Relay (Recommended)
92	
93	```bash
94	# Using strfry
95	docker run -p 7777:7777 ghcr.io/hoytech/strfry
96	
97	# Configure openclaw to use local relay
98	"relays": ["ws://localhost:7777"]
99	```
100	
101	### Manual Test
102	
103	1. Start the gateway with Nostr configured
104	2. Open Damus, Amethyst, or another Nostr client
105	3. Send a DM to your bot's npub
106	4. Verify the bot responds
107	
108	## Protocol Support
109	
110	| NIP    | Status    | Notes                  |
111	| ------ | --------- | ---------------------- |
112	| NIP-01 | Supported | Basic event structure  |
113	| NIP-04 | Supported | Encrypted DMs (kind:4) |
114	| NIP-17 | Planned   | Gift-wrapped DMs (v2)  |
115	
116	## Security Notes
117	
118	- Private keys are never logged
119	- Event signatures are verified before processing
120	- Sender policy is checked before expensive crypto work
121	- Inbound DMs are rate-limited and oversized payloads are dropped before decrypt
122	- Use environment variables for keys, never commit to config files
123	- Consider using `allowlist` mode in production
124	
125	## Troubleshooting
126	
127	### Bot not receiving messages
128	
129	1. Verify private key is correctly configured
130	2. Check relay connectivity
131	3. Ensure `enabled` is not set to `false`
132	4. Check the bot's public key matches what you're sending to
133	
134	### Messages not being delivered
135	
136	1. Check relay URLs are correct (must use `wss://`)
137	2. Verify relays are online and accepting connections
138	3. Check for rate limiting (reduce message frequency)
139	
140	## License
141	
142	MIT
143

## OpenClaw Bundled Channel Plugins Structure Report

I've thoroughly explored the OpenClaw repository's bundled channel plugins structure. Here's what I found:

### Repository Structure

**Workspace Configuration:** `pnpm-workspace.yaml`
- Includes: `.`, `ui`, `packages/*`, and `extensions/*`
- The `extensions/` directory contains all bundled plugins (bundled and third-party installable)

### All 22 Bundled Channel Plugins

These plugins all have a `channels` field in their `openclaw.plugin.json`:

1. **IRC** - `extensions/irc/`
2. **Nostr** - `extensions/nostr/`
3. **Telegram** - `extensions/telegram/`
4. **Matrix** - `extensions/matrix/`
5. Discord - `extensions/discord/`
6. Slack - `extensions/slack/`
7. Signal - `extensions/signal/`
8. WhatsApp - `extensions/whatsapp/`
9. Twitch - `extensions/twitch/`
10. Tlon - `extensions/tlon/`
11. Synology Chat - `extensions/synology-chat/`
12. MSTeams - `extensions/msteams/`
13. Mattermost - `extensions/mattermost/`
14. Line - `extensions/line/`
15. iMessage - `extensions/imessage/`
16. Google Chat - `extensions/googlechat/`
17. Feishu - `extensions/feishu/`
18. QQ Bot - `extensions/qqbot/`
19. Nextcloud Talk - `extensions/nextcloud-talk/`
20. Zalo - `extensions/zalo/`
21. Zalo User - `extensions/zalouser/`
22. BlueBubbles - `extensions/bluebubbles/`

### Detailed Structure of Four Main Plugins

#### 1. **IRC** (`extensions/irc/`) - 40 files

**Root files (API/entry points):**
- `index.ts` - Main plugin entry
- `api.ts` - Public API exports
- `channel-config-api.ts` - Channel configuration API
- `setup-entry.ts` - Setup/onboarding entry point
- `runtime-api.ts` - Runtime API
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - Plugin manifest
- `package.json` - NPM manifest

**Source code (`src/` - 36 TypeScript files):**
- `channel.ts` - Core channel implementation
- `client.ts` - IRC client wrapper
- `runtime.ts` - Runtime configuration
- `accounts.ts` - Account management
- `config-schema.ts` - Configuration schema
- `config-ui-hints.ts` - UI hints for configuration
- `inbound.ts` - Inbound message handling
- `send.ts` - Outbound message sending
- `protocol.ts` - IRC protocol handling
- `connect-options.ts` - Connection options
- `control-chars.ts` - Control character handling
- `normalize.ts` - Message normalization
- `policy.ts` - Access policies
- `probe.ts` - Connection probing
- `monitor.ts` - Connection monitoring
- `setup-core.ts` - Core setup logic
- `setup-surface.ts` - Setup UI surface
- `types.ts` - TypeScript type definitions
- Plus 18 test files (`*.test.ts`)

#### 2. **Nostr** (`extensions/nostr/`) - 38 files

**Root files:**
- `index.ts` - Main plugin entry with HTTP handler registration
- `api.ts` - Public API
- `setup-entry.ts` - Setup entry
- `setup-api.ts` - Setup API
- `runtime-api.ts` - Runtime API
- `test-api.ts` - Test API
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - Plugin manifest
- `package.json` - NPM manifest with `nostr-tools` dependency
- `README.md` - Plugin documentation
- `CHANGELOG.md` - Version history

**Source code (`src/` - 24 TypeScript files + `test/` folder):**
- `channel.ts` - Core channel implementation
- `runtime.ts` - Runtime management
- `config-schema.ts` - Configuration schema
- `nostr-bus.ts` - Nostr bus/relay management (with test and integration test)
- `nostr-profile.ts` - Profile management
- `nostr-profile-http.ts` - HTTP profile handler
- `nostr-profile-import.ts` - Profile import functionality
- `nostr-state-store.ts` - State persistence
- `default-relays.ts` - Default relay configuration
- `session-route.ts` - Session routing
- `seen-tracker.ts` - Event deduplication
- `setup-surface.ts` - Setup UI surface
- `metrics.ts` - Metrics/logging
- `types.ts` - Type definitions
- Plus 7+ test files (`.test.ts`, `.inbound.test.ts`, `.outbound.test.ts`, `.fuzz.test.ts`, `.integration.test.ts`)

#### 3. **Telegram** (`extensions/telegram/`) - 212 files

**Root files:**
- `index.ts` - Main plugin entry
- `api.ts` - Public API
- `channel-config-api.ts` - Configuration API
- `runtime-api.ts` - Runtime API
- `session-key-api.ts` - Session key API
- `allow-from.ts` - Access control utilities
- `setup-entry.ts` - Setup entry
- `test-api.ts` - Test utilities
- `test-support.ts` - Test support helpers
- `update-offset-runtime-api.ts` - Offset management API
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - Plugin manifest
- `package.json` - With dependencies: grammy, @grammyjs/runner, @grammyjs/transformer-throttler

**Source code (`src/` - ~192 TypeScript files):**
- Multiple functional modules for accounts, actions, bot, channel, config, handlers, message formatting, relay, routing, session management, setup, state, types, and utilities
- Extensive test coverage

#### 4. **Matrix** (`extensions/matrix/`) - 251 files

**Root files:**
- `index.ts` - Main plugin entry with test
- `api.ts` - Public API
- `helper-api.ts` - Helper functions API
- `runtime-api.ts` - Runtime API
- `setup-entry.ts` - Setup entry
- `test-api.ts` - Test utilities
- `plugin-entry.handlers.runtime.ts` - Handler runtime configuration
- `thread-bindings-runtime.ts` - Thread binding management
- `legacy-crypto-inspector.ts` - Legacy encryption support
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - Plugin manifest
- `package.json` - With matrix-js-sdk (v41.3.0-rc.0), crypto libraries, markdown-it
- `CHANGELOG.md` - Version history

**Source code (`src/` - ~53 TypeScript files):**
- Multiple modules for account selection, account propagation, actions, bot management, channel implementation, config, crypto, device verification, handlers, message formatting, room management, setup, state, sync, types, and utilities
- Comprehensive test coverage including fuzz tests

### Plugin Manifest Files

All manifests follow this pattern:

**IRC `openclaw.plugin.json`:**
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**IRC `package.json` excerpt:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw IRC channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls.",
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

**Nostr `package.json` excerpt:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/nostr",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw Nostr channel plugin for NIP-04 encrypted DMs",
  "dependencies": {
    "nostr-tools": "^2.23.3"
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "channel": {
      "id": "nostr",
      "label": "Nostr",
      "selectionLabel": "Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)",
      "blurb": "Decentralized protocol; encrypted DMs via NIP-04.",
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true
    },
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/nostr",
      "publishToNpm": true,
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    }
  }
}
```

### Extensions Boundary Documentation

**File:** `extensions/AGENTS.md` (symlinked from `extensions/CLAUDE.md`)

Key points about bundled plugins:
- Plugins in `extensions/` follow the same boundary rules as third-party plugins
- **Public Contracts:**
  - Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths
  - Use plugin-facing contracts: `plugin-entry.ts`, `core.ts`, `provider-entry.ts`, `channel-contract.ts`
  - Reference docs in `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`

- **Boundary Rules:**
  - No imports from core internals (`src/**`, `src/channels/**`, `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`)
  - No relative imports escaping package root
  - Keep `openclaw.plugin.json` metadata accurate
  - Treat `src/**` as private unless exported through `api.ts`

### Root Level Directory Structure

- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` - Workspace configuration
- `extensions/` - All plugins (94 total, 22 with channels)
- Other key directories: `packages/`, `ui/`, `src/`, `scripts/`, `skills/`, `apps/`

### Key Files Relative to Repo Root

- `pnpm-workspace.yaml`
- `extensions/AGENTS.md` - Boundary documentation
- `extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/irc/package.json`
- `extensions/nostr/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/nostr/package.json`
- `extensions/nostr/README.md`
- `extensions/telegram/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/telegram/package.json`
- `extensions/matrix/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/matrix/package.json`
- `extensions/matrix/CHANGELOG.md`

The bundled plugins follow a consistent structure with root-level entry points (index.ts, api.ts, setup-entry.ts) and a src/ directory containing implementation details. All plugins use the plugin SDK contract for defining channels, and many include extensive test coverage.

- Multiple functional modules for accounts, actions, bot, channel, config, handlers, message formatting, relay, routing, session management, setup, state, types, and utilities
- Extensive test coverage

#### 4. **Matrix** (`extensions/matrix/`) - 251 files

**Root files:**
- `index.ts` - Main plugin entry with test
- `api.ts` - Public API
- `helper-api.ts` - Helper functions API
- `runtime-api.ts` - Runtime API
- `setup-entry.ts` - Setup entry
- `test-api.ts` - Test utilities
- `plugin-entry.handlers.runtime.ts` - Handler runtime configuration
- `thread-bindings-runtime.ts` - Thread binding management
- `legacy-crypto-inspector.ts` - Legacy encryption support
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - Plugin manifest
- `package.json` - With matrix-js-sdk (v41.3.0-rc.0), crypto libraries, markdown-it
- `CHANGELOG.md` - Version history

**Source code (`src/` - ~53 TypeScript files):**
- Multiple modules for account selection, account propagation, actions, bot management, channel implementation, config, crypto, device verification, handlers, message formatting, room management, setup, state, sync, types, and utilities
- Comprehensive test coverage including fuzz tests

### Plugin Manifest Files

All manifests follow this pattern:

**IRC `openclaw.plugin.json`:**
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**IRC `package.json` excerpt:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw IRC channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls.",
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

**Nostr `package.json` excerpt:**
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/nostr",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw Nostr channel plugin for NIP-04 encrypted DMs",
  "dependencies": {
    "nostr-tools": "^2.23.3"
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "channel": {
      "id": "nostr",
      "label": "Nostr",
      "selectionLabel": "Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)",
      "blurb": "Decentralized protocol; encrypted DMs via NIP-04.",
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true
    },
    "install": {
      "npmSpec": "@openclaw/nostr",
      "publishToNpm": true,
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    }
  }
}
```

### Extensions Boundary Documentation

**File:** `extensions/AGENTS.md` (symlinked from `extensions/CLAUDE.md`)

Key points about bundled plugins:
- Plugins in `extensions/` follow the same boundary rules as third-party plugins
- **Public Contracts:**
  - Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths
  - Use plugin-facing contracts: `plugin-entry.ts`, `core.ts`, `provider-entry.ts`, `channel-contract.ts`
  - Reference docs in `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`

- **Boundary Rules:**
  - No imports from core internals (`src/**`, `src/channels/**`, `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`)
  - No relative imports escaping package root
  - Keep `openclaw.plugin.json` metadata accurate
  - Treat `src/**` as private unless exported through `api.ts`

### Root Level Directory Structure

- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` - Workspace configuration
- `extensions/` - All plugins (94 total, 22 with channels)
- Other key directories: `packages/`, `ui/`, `src/`, `scripts/`, `skills/`, `apps/`

### Key Files Relative to Repo Root

- `pnpm-workspace.yaml`
- `extensions/AGENTS.md` - Boundary documentation
- `extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/irc/package.json`
- `extensions/nostr/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/nostr/package.json`
- `extensions/nostr/README.md`
- `extensions/telegram/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/telegram/package.json`
- `extensions/matrix/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `extensions/matrix/package.json`
- `extensions/matrix/CHANGELOG.md`

The bundled plugins follow a consistent structure with root-level entry points (index.ts, api.ts, setup-entry.ts) and a src/ directory containing implementation details. All plugins use the plugin SDK contract for defining channels, and many include extensive test coverage.

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. The available SDK subpaths include:
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound` - has `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/direct-dm` - has `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime`, `resolveInboundDirectDmAccessWithRuntime`, `createPreCryptoDirectDmAuthorizer`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-send-result` - has `attachChannelToResult`


- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store` - has `createPluginRuntimeStore`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` - has `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`, `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter`, and related utilities
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives` - has `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, and other schema definitions
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers` - has `createAccountListHelpers`, `describeAccountSnapshot`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id` - has `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID` and related account identifiers
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution` - handles account config merging and default account resolution
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input` - manages secret input schemas and normalization
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` - the main entry point
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup` - setup utilities
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-setup` - provides channel setup adapters and wizards
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing` - manages channel pairing controllers
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` - creates account status adapters and runtime state
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/extension-shared` - shared extension utilities
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime` - runtime configuration types
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing` - routing utilities including account ID normalization
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod` - Zod validation
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy` - warning collection
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/directory-runtime` - directory adapter
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/allow-from` - allow-from entry formatting

Since PulseBoard is a new fictional plugin without its own SDK module, the `runtime-api.ts` barrel should pull from these generic SDK paths instead.

Now I need to check what the nostr channel's SDK entry exports to understand what pattern I should follow for PulseBoard's barrel exports.

Now I have a thorough understanding. Let me now design the PulseBoard plugin. 

Key findings:
1. The `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr` is a **bundled plugin-specific SDK helper** for Nostr. It doesn't exist for PulseBoard since PulseBoard is a new fictional plugin.
2. For PulseBoard's `runtime-api.ts`, I should import directly from the generic SDK subpaths like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/direct-dm`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, etc.


3. The `runtime-api.ts` at the root level serves as the central export point for SDK utilities that the extension's source files need, so they only import locally from there.

I'm noticing the Nostr extension has its `runtime-api.ts` at the root level rather than inside `src/`, and the source files reference it with relative paths. Let me verify how this pattern applies to other extensions like IRC.

The location of the barrel file varies by plugin—Nostr keeps it at the root and re-exports from the SDK, while IRC places it inside `src/`. For PulseBoard without a dedicated SDK, the `runtime-api.ts` would need to aggregate imports from multiple generic SDK subpaths instead.

Now I'm mapping out the complete structure for the PulseBoard plugin, which is a real-time bulletin board platform supporting both group boards and direct messages with a 4000-character limit. The configuration will handle authentication, server connection, username, DM policies, allowed senders, and board-specific settings. I need to create the plugin files including the manifest, package configuration, entry point, API barrel, and setup utilities.

The core implementation will be organized across several modules: channel management for handling both direct and board messages, type definitions, configuration schema validation, runtime initialization, account management, setup procedures for both core and UI surfaces, message normalization, sending logic, monitoring, and tests. Key design decisions include using `@username` or `#board-name` format for addressing, API key authentication, and structured configuration for access control. I'm deciding between putting the runtime-api barrel at the root level or in src, and I'll go with root-level like Nostr for simplicity. Now I need to identify all the exports that runtime-api.ts should provide—types like ChannelPlugin and OpenClawConfig, utility functions for handling direct messages and authorization, schema builders, and controller factories.

Looking at how Nostr's runtime-api works, it just re-exports everything from a single plugin-sdk entry point, but PulseBoard doesn't have that consolidated export yet. I'll need to selectively import from the individual generic paths instead, pulling PluginRuntime from the shared runtime types and other utilities from their respective modules. I'm realizing the simpler approach is to skip the root-level `runtime-api.ts` that re-exports from multiple SDK paths, and instead have each file import directly from the SDK as needed.

But looking at how IRC and Nostr both use a `runtime-api.ts` barrel file, option 2 seems cleaner—creating a thin barrel that aggregates exports from multiple SDK paths rather than scattering imports throughout the codebase. I need to check what Nostr's channel file actually imports to understand what the barrel needs to expose. I'm mapping out where each of these utilities comes from across the SDK subpaths—most are in direct-dm or status-helpers, though a few like the pairing hint formatter are less certain, and the ChannelPlugin type lives in the core module. I'm realizing I don't need to pin down every import path right now—the plan should just outline which files to create and their responsibilities, then I can determine the exact SDK subpaths by following existing plugin patterns or checking each export individually. For the aggregation file, I'll map out the generic SDK paths it should pull from like `openclaw/plugin-sdk/direct-dm`, `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-inbound`, and the other helper modules, leaving room to verify the exact locations as I implement.

Actually, a simpler approach makes more sense: instead of creating a barrel file that sources from multiple places, I can have `src/channel.ts` import directly from the appropriate SDK subpaths, which aligns with the boundary rules allowing extension code to import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and local barrels. This keeps the plan cleaner and the implementation more straightforward.

Now I'm designing PulseBoard as a plugin with a simpler architecture similar to Nostr but supporting both direct messages to users and group board conversations like IRC, authenticated via API key with WebSocket-based real-time messaging and a 4000 character message limit.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new "PulseBoard" fictional channel plugin in OpenClaw. This is a new extension to be placed at `extensions/pulseboard/`. Do NOT plan any modifications to existing files.

## Background

OpenClaw uses a plugin system where channel plugins live in `extensions/<id>/`. The full extension boundary rules are:
- Extensions import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths and their own local barrels
- No imports from core `src/**` or other extension `src/**`
- No relative imports escaping the package root

## PulseBoard Platform Specification
PulseBoard is a fictional real-time bulletin board messaging platform:
- Users post to named "boards" (group chats) identified as `#board-name`  
- Users can also send direct messages (identified as `@username` or just `username`)
- 4000-character text chunk limit
- API key + server URL + username for authentication
- Real-time WebSocket-style monitoring for inbound messages (we'll stub it since it's fictional)
- Both `direct` and `group` chat types supported

## Available SDK Subpaths (all valid as `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<name>`)
From `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`, the relevant ones are:
- `core` → `createChatChannelPlugin`, `defineChannelPluginEntry`, `defineSetupPluginEntry`
- `direct-dm` → `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime`, `resolveInboundDirectDmAccessWithRuntime`, `createPreCryptoDirectDmAuthorizer`
- `channel-inbound` → also exports `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime`
- `channel-config-helpers` → `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`, `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter`, `adaptScopedAccountAccessor`, `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`
- `channel-config-primitives` → `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `MarkdownConfigSchema`, `AllowFromListSchema`
- `channel-config-schema` → `buildChannelConfigSchema`
- `account-helpers` → `createAccountListHelpers`, `describeAccountSnapshot`
- `account-id` → `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId`
- `account-resolution` → `resolveMergedAccountConfig`, `resolveListedDefaultAccountId`
- `secret-input` → `buildSecretInputSchema`, `normalizeSecretInputString`, `hasConfiguredSecretInput`, `normalizeSecretInputString`
- `runtime-store` → `createPluginRuntimeStore`
- `setup` → `patchTopLevelChannelConfigSection`, `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicy`, `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `formatDocsLink`, `mergeAllowFromEntries`, `splitSetupEntries`, `createTopLevelChannelParsedAllowFromPrompt`, `parseSetupEntriesWithParser`, `setSetupChannelEnabled`, `createAllowFromSection`
- `channel-setup` → `ChannelSetupAdapter`, `ChannelSetupWizard`, `ChannelSetupDmPolicy`
- `channel-pairing` → `createChannelPairingController`
- `channel-send-result` → `attachChannelToResult`
- `status-helpers` → `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`, `createDefaultChannelRuntimeState`
- `extension-shared` → `buildPassiveChannelStatusSummary`, `buildTrafficStatusSummary`, `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`
- `channel-policy` → warning collector helpers
- `directory-runtime` → `createChannelDirectoryAdapter`, `createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister`
- `allow-from` → `formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries`
- `routing` → `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`
- `zod` → Zod
- `config-runtime` → `OpenClawConfig`, `DmPolicy`
- `channel-contract` → `ChannelPlugin` type
- `plugin-runtime` or `runtime` → `PluginRuntime` type

## Key Patterns from Existing Plugins

### Nostr (closest match for single-account, direct-only → extend to groups)
**File layout (root level):**
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - minimal manifest `{"id": "pulseboard", "channels": ["pulseboard"], "configSchema": {...}}`
- `package.json` - npm config with `openclaw` block
- `index.ts` - `defineChannelPluginEntry({id, name, description, plugin, setRuntime})`
- `api.ts` - `export * from "./runtime-api.js"`
- `setup-entry.ts` - `export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)`
- `runtime-api.ts` - aggregates SDK re-exports needed by src/ files

**src/ files:**
- `channel.ts` - main `createChatChannelPlugin({base, pairing, security, outbound})`
- `types.ts` - `PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage`
- `config-schema.ts` - Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema`
- `runtime.ts` - `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized")`
- `accounts.ts` - `resolvePulseBoardAccount`, `listPulseBoardAccountIds` using `createAccountListHelpers`
- `setup-core.ts` - `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter`
- `setup-surface.ts` - `pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`
- `normalize.ts` - `normalizePulseBoardTarget`, `looksLikePulseBoardTarget`, `isBoardTarget`, `normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry`
- `send.ts` - `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts)` returning `{to, messageId}`
- `monitor.ts` - `monitorPulseBoardAccount({accountId, config, runtime, abortSignal, statusSink, onMessage})` — stub/placeholder
- `channel.test.ts` - Vitest tests for basic plugin metadata, capabilities, chunking

### IRC channel.ts pattern for groups
```typescript
export const pulseBoardPlugin = createChatChannelPlugin({
  base: {
    id: "pulseboard",
    meta: { id: "pulseboard", label: "PulseBoard", selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (Bulletin Board)", docsPath: "/channels/pulseboard", blurb: "...", quickstartAllowFrom: true },
    setup: pulseBoardSetupAdapter,
    setupWizard: pulseBoardSetupWizard,
    capabilities: { chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: false },
    reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"] },
    configSchema: PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema,
    config: { ...pulseBoardConfigAdapter, isConfigured: (account) => account.configured, describeAccount: ... },
    groups: { resolveRequireMention, resolveToolPolicy },
    messaging: { normalizeTarget: normalizePulseBoardTarget, targetResolver: { looksLikeId, hint: "<#board-name|@username>" } },
    resolver: { resolveTargets: ... },
    directory: createChannelDirectoryAdapter({ listPeers, listGroups }),
    status: createComputedAccountStatusAdapter(...),
    gateway: { startAccount: async (ctx) => { ... await runStoppablePassiveMonitor(...) } },
  },
  pairing: { text: { idLabel: "pulseBoardUser", message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE, normalizeAllowEntry, notify } },
  security: { resolveDmPolicy: resolvePulseBoardDmPolicy },
  outbound: { base: { deliveryMode: "direct", textChunkLimit: 4000 }, attachedResults: { channel: "pulseboard", sendText, sendMedia } }
});
```

## What to Plan

Please provide a step-by-step implementation plan with:
1. Complete file list and their purposes
2. For each file, the key code structure and notable imports
3. How targets are normalized (# prefix for boards, no prefix for DM users)
4. How the config schema looks (apiKey, serverUrl, username, dmPolicy, allowFrom, boards map, markdown)  
5. How accounts.ts resolves the account (env var support: PULSEBOARD_API_KEY, PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL, PULSEBOARD_USERNAME)
6. How monitor.ts is structured as a placeholder
7. How setup-surface.ts wizard works
8. What the runtime-api.ts barrel should export and from which SDK subpaths
9. Test file structure

Be very specific about imports and code structure. This plan will be directly executed to create the files.

- `types.ts` - `PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage`
- `config-schema.ts` - Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema`
- `runtime.ts` - `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized")`
- `accounts.ts` - `resolvePulseBoardAccount`, `listPulseBoardAccountIds` using `createAccountListHelpers`
- `setup-core.ts` - `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter`
- `setup-surface.ts` - `pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`
- `normalize.ts` - `normalizePulseBoardTarget`, `looksLikePulseBoardTarget`, `isBoardTarget`, `normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry`
- `send.ts` - `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts)` returning `{to, messageId}`
- `monitor.ts` - `monitorPulseBoardAccount({accountId, config, runtime, abortSignal, statusSink, onMessage})` — stub/placeholder
- `channel.test.ts` - Vitest tests for basic plugin metadata, capabilities, chunking

### IRC channel.ts pattern for groups
```typescript
export const pulseBoardPlugin = createChatChannelPlugin({
  base: {
    id: "pulseboard",
    meta: { id: "pulseboard", label: "PulseBoard", selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (Bulletin Board)", docsPath: "/channels/pulseboard", blurb: "...", quickstartAllowFrom: true },
    setup: pulseBoardSetupAdapter,
    setupWizard: pulseBoardSetupWizard,
    capabilities: { chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: false },
    reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"] },
    configSchema: PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema,
    config: { ...pulseBoardConfigAdapter, isConfigured: (account) => account.configured, describeAccount: ... },
    groups: { resolveRequireMention, resolveToolPolicy },
    messaging: { normalizeTarget: normalizePulseBoardTarget, targetResolver: { looksLikeId, hint: "<#board-name|@username>" } },
    resolver: { resolveTargets: ... },
    directory: createChannelDirectoryAdapter({ listPeers, listGroups }),
    status: createComputedAccountStatusAdapter(...),
    gateway: { startAccount: async (ctx) => { ... await runStoppablePassiveMonitor(...) } },
  },
  pairing: { text: { idLabel: "pulseBoardUser", message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE, normalizeAllowEntry, notify } },
  security: { resolveDmPolicy: resolvePulseBoardDmPolicy },
  outbound: { base: { deliveryMode: "direct", textChunkLimit: 4000 }, attachedResults: { channel: "pulseboard", sendText, sendMedia } }
});
```

## What to Plan

Please provide a step-by-step implementation plan with:
1. Complete file list and their purposes
2. For each file, the key code structure and notable imports
3. How targets are normalized (# prefix for boards, no prefix for DM users)
4. How the config schema looks (apiKey, serverUrl, username, dmPolicy, allowFrom, boards map, markdown)  
5. How accounts.ts resolves the account (env var support: PULSEBOARD_API_KEY, PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL, PULSEBOARD_USERNAME)
6. How monitor.ts is structured as a placeholder
7. How setup-surface.ts wizard works
8. What the runtime-api.ts barrel should export and from which SDK subpaths
9. Test file structure

Be very specific about imports and code structure. This plan will be directly executed to create the files.

1	import {
2	  AllowFromListSchema,
3	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
4	  DmPolicySchema,
5	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
6	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";
7	import { buildSecretInputSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input";
8	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
9	
10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);

27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });

50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new "PulseBoard" fictional channel plugin in OpenClaw. This is a new extension to be placed at `extensions/pulseboard/`. Do NOT plan any modifications to existing files.

## Background

OpenClaw uses a plugin system where channel plugins live in `extensions/<id>/`. The full extension boundary rules are:
- Extensions import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths and their own local barrels
- No imports from core `src/**` or other extension `src/**`
- No relative imports escaping the package root

## PulseBoard Platform Specification
PulseBoard is a fictional real-time bulletin board messaging platform:
- Users post to named "boards" (group chats) identified as `#board-name`  
- Users can also send direct messages (identified as `@username` or just `username`)
- 4000-character text chunk limit
- API key + server URL + username for authentication
- Real-time WebSocket-style monitoring for inbound messages (we'll stub it since it's fictional)
- Both `direct` and `group` chat types supported

## Available SDK Subpaths (all valid as `openclaw/plugin-sdk/<name>`)
From `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`, the relevant ones are:
- `core` → `createChatChannelPlugin`, `defineChannelPluginEntry`, `defineSetupPluginEntry`
- `direct-dm` → `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime`, `resolveInboundDirectDmAccessWithRuntime`, `createPreCryptoDirectDmAuthorizer`
- `channel-inbound` → also exports `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime`
- `channel-config-helpers` → `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`, `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter`, `adaptScopedAccountAccessor`, `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`
- `channel-config-primitives` → `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `MarkdownConfigSchema`, `AllowFromListSchema`
- `channel-config-schema` → `buildChannelConfigSchema`
- `account-helpers` → `createAccountListHelpers`, `describeAccountSnapshot`
- `account-id` → `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId`
- `account-resolution` → `resolveMergedAccountConfig`, `resolveListedDefaultAccountId`
- `secret-input` → `buildSecretInputSchema`, `normalizeSecretInputString`, `hasConfiguredSecretInput`, `normalizeSecretInputString`
- `runtime-store` → `createPluginRuntimeStore`
- `setup` → `patchTopLevelChannelConfigSection`, `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicy`, `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `formatDocsLink`, `mergeAllowFromEntries`, `splitSetupEntries`, `createTopLevelChannelParsedAllowFromPrompt`, `parseSetupEntriesWithParser`, `setSetupChannelEnabled`, `createAllowFromSection`
- `channel-setup` → `ChannelSetupAdapter`, `ChannelSetupWizard`, `ChannelSetupDmPolicy`
- `channel-pairing` → `createChannelPairingController`
- `channel-send-result` → `attachChannelToResult`
- `status-helpers` → `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`, `createDefaultChannelRuntimeState`
- `extension-shared` → `buildPassiveChannelStatusSummary`, `buildTrafficStatusSummary`, `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`
- `channel-policy` → warning collector helpers
- `directory-runtime` → `createChannelDirectoryAdapter`, `createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister`
- `allow-from` → `formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries`
- `routing` → `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`
- `zod` → Zod
- `config-runtime` → `OpenClawConfig`, `DmPolicy`
- `channel-contract` → `ChannelPlugin` type
- `plugin-runtime` or `runtime` → `PluginRuntime` type

## Key Patterns from Existing Plugins

### Nostr (closest match for single-account, direct-only → extend to groups)
**File layout (root level):**
- `openclaw.plugin.json` - minimal manifest `{"id": "pulseboard", "channels": ["pulseboard"], "configSchema": {...}}`
- `package.json` - npm config with `openclaw` block
- `index.ts` - `defineChannelPluginEntry({id, name, description, plugin, setRuntime})`
- `api.ts` - `export * from "./runtime-api.js"`
- `setup-entry.ts` - `export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)`
- `runtime-api.ts` - aggregates SDK re-exports needed by src/ files

**src/ files:**
- `channel.ts` - main `createChatChannelPlugin({base, pairing, security, outbound})`
- `types.ts` - `PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage`
- `config-schema.ts` - Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema`
- `runtime.ts` - `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized")`
- `accounts.ts` - `resolvePulseBoardAccount`, `listPulseBoardAccountIds` using `createAccountListHelpers`
- `setup-core.ts` - `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter`
- `setup-surface.ts` - `pulseBoardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`
- `normalize.ts` - `normalizePulseBoardTarget`, `looksLikePulseBoardTarget`, `isBoardTarget`, `normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry`
- `send.ts` - `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts)` returning `{to, messageId}`
- `monitor.ts` - `monitorPulseBoardAccount({accountId, config, runtime, abortSignal, statusSink, onMessage})` — stub/placeholder
- `channel.test.ts` - Vitest tests for basic plugin metadata, capabilities, chunking

### IRC channel.ts pattern for groups
```typescript
export const pulseBoardPlugin = createChatChannelPlugin({
  base: {
    id: "pulseboard",
    meta: { id: "pulseboard", label: "PulseBoard", selectionLabel: "PulseBoard (Bulletin Board)", docsPath: "/channels/pulseboard", blurb: "...", quickstartAllowFrom: true },
    setup: pulseBoardSetupAdapter,
    setupWizard: pulseBoardSetupWizard,
    capabilities: { chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: false },
    reload: { configPrefixes: ["channels.pulseboard"] },
    configSchema: PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema,
    config: { ...pulseBoardConfigAdapter, isConfigured: (account) => account.configured, describeAccount: ... },
    groups: { resolveRequireMention, resolveToolPolicy },
    messaging: { normalizeTarget: normalizePulseBoardTarget, targetResolver: { looksLikeId, hint: "<#board-name|@username>" } },
    resolver: { resolveTargets: ... },
    directory: createChannelDirectoryAdapter({ listPeers, listGroups }),
    status: createComputedAccountStatusAdapter(...),
    gateway: { startAccount: async (ctx) => { ... await runStoppablePassiveMonitor(...) } },
  },
  pairing: { text: { idLabel: "pulseBoardUser", message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE, normalizeAllowEntry, notify } },
  security: { resolveDmPolicy: resolvePulseBoardDmPolicy },
  outbound: { base: { deliveryMode: "direct", textChunkLimit: 4000 }, attachedResults: { channel: "pulseboard", sendText, sendMedia } }
});
```

## What to Plan

Please provide a step-by-step implementation plan with:
1. Complete file list and their purposes
2. For each file, the key code structure and notable imports
3. How targets are normalized (# prefix for boards, no prefix for DM users)
4. How the config schema looks (apiKey, serverUrl, username, dmPolicy, allowFrom, boards map, markdown)  
5. How accounts.ts resolves the account (env var support: PULSEBOARD_API_KEY, PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL, PULSEBOARD_USERNAME)
6. How monitor.ts is structured as a placeholder
7. How setup-surface.ts wizard works
8. What the runtime-api.ts barrel should export and from which SDK subpaths
9. Test file structure

Be very specific about imports and code structure. This plan will be directly executed to create the files.

## PulseBoard Channel Plugin Plan

I've thoroughly analyzed the codebase (IRC, Nostr, Telegram, Signal plugins + plugin SDK contracts) and created a detailed implementation plan. Here's the summary:

### What gets created: 17 new files in `extensions/pulseboard/`

**Root level (6 files):**
- `openclaw.plugin.json` — minimal discovery manifest `{"id":"pulseboard","channels":["pulseboard"],...}`
- `package.json` — `@openclaw/pulseboard`, no production deps (fictional platform), openclaw metadata block
- `index.ts` — `defineChannelPluginEntry({id:"pulseboard",...})`
- `api.ts` — `export * from "./runtime-api.js"`
- `setup-entry.ts` — `export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)`
- `runtime-api.ts` — aggregates re-exports from generic SDK subpaths (`direct-dm`, `status-helpers`, `extension-shared`, `channel-send-result`, `channel-pairing`, `channel-config-primitives`, etc.) since there's no dedicated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard`

**`src/` (11 files):**
- `types.ts` — `PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, `CoreConfig`
- `config-schema.ts` — Zod schema with `apiKey` (SecretInput), `serverUrl`, `username`, `dmPolicy`, `allowFrom`, `boards` map; wrapped with `buildChannelConfigSchema`
- `accounts.ts` — single-account resolution (like Nostr); env vars: `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`
- `runtime.ts` — `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized")`
- `normalize.ts` — `normalizePulseBoardTarget` (`#board-name` for groups, bare `username` for DMs), `isBoardTarget`, `looksLikePulseBoardTargetId`, `normalizePulseBoardAllowEntry`
- `send.ts` — `sendMessagePulseBoard(to, text, opts)` stub (fictional platform, logs activity)
- `monitor.ts` — placeholder `monitorPulseBoardAccount(...)` that parks until abort signal (documented with real WebSocket implementation notes)
- `setup-core.ts` — `pulseBoardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter` using `patchTopLevelChannelConfigSection`
- `setup-surface.ts` — `pulseBoardSetupWizard` with credential (API key), textInputs (server URL, username), dmPolicy, envShortcut
- `channel.ts` — `createChatChannelPlugin({base:{capabilities:{chatTypes:["direct","group"]}, textChunkLimit:4000,...}, pairing, security, outbound})`
- `channel.test.ts` — Vitest tests for metadata, capabilities, chunk limit

### Key design decisions:
- **Single-account** (top-level config, like Nostr — no accounts map)
- **4000-char chunk limit** as specified
- **Both direct + group** chat types
- **DM pairing** default policy
- **No production npm deps** (fictional platform)
- `outbound` uses IRC-style `base:`/`attachedResults:` nesting within `createChatChannelPlugin`

### Verification after implementation:
```
pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.test.ts
pnpm check
pnpm build
```

input.summary:
I've thoroughly analyzed the codebase — reading IRC, Nostr, Telegram, and Signal channel plugin implementations alongside the full plugin SDK contracts — and produced a detailed implementation plan for the PulseBoard extension.

**17 new files** will be created under `extensions/pulseboard/`, zero existing files modified.

Key highlights:
- **Single-account architecture** (like Nostr) with top-level `channels.pulseboard.*` config
- **Both `direct` + `group` chat types** (boards = `#board-name`, DMs = `username`)
- **4000-char text chunk limit** as specified
- **API key + server URL + username** for authentication; env vars `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME` supported
- **`runtime-api.ts`** imports from generic SDK subpaths (`direct-dm`, `status-helpers`, `extension-shared`, `channel-send-result`, etc.) since there's no dedicated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard`
- **Placeholder monitor** (`monitor.ts`) that parks until abort — documented with notes for where real WebSocket code would go
- **Full wizard** with API key credential, serverUrl + username text inputs, dmPolicy, allowFrom sections
- **Vitest tests** covering plugin id, capabilities, and chunk limit

Two implementation-time verifications flagged: confirm `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE` export location (likely `channel-pairing` or `channel-plugin-common`) and confirm whether `createChatChannelPlugin` outbound uses IRC-style `base:`/`attachedResults:` nesting vs Nostr-style flat form.

The plan file has been written to `/Users/timpietrusky/.claude/plans/mutable-wondering-quill.md`. Here's a summary of what the plan covers:

---

## PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Plan Summary

**17 new files** under `extensions/pulseboard/`. Zero existing files modified.

### File Structure
```
extensions/pulseboard/
├── openclaw.plugin.json      ← discovery manifest
├── package.json              ← @openclaw/pulseboard, no prod deps
├── index.ts                  ← defineChannelPluginEntry
├── api.ts                    ← export * from ./runtime-api.js
├── setup-entry.ts            ← defineSetupPluginEntry
├── runtime-api.ts            ← aggregated re-exports from generic SDK subpaths
└── src/
    ├── types.ts              ← PulseBoardAccountConfig, ResolvedPulseBoardAccount, etc.
    ├── config-schema.ts      ← Zod schema (apiKey, serverUrl, username, dmPolicy, allowFrom, boards)
    ├── runtime.ts            ← createPluginRuntimeStore
    ├── accounts.ts           ← single-account resolver + env var support
    ├── normalize.ts          ← #board-name (group) vs username (DM) normalization
    ├── send.ts               ← sendMessagePulseBoard stub
    ├── monitor.ts            ← placeholder inbound monitor (WebSocket stub)
    ├── setup-core.ts         ← ChannelSetupAdapter
    ├── setup-surface.ts      ← ChannelSetupWizard with credentials + textInputs
    ├── channel.ts            ← createChatChannelPlugin (the main plugin definition)
    └── channel.test.ts       ← Vitest tests
```

### Key Design Decisions
| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Account model | Single-account (like Nostr), top-level `channels.pulseboard.*` |
| Chat types | `["direct", "group"]` |
| Group target format | `#board-name` |
| DM target format | `username` (strips `@`) |
| Text chunk limit | **4000 characters** |
| Auth | `apiKey` (SecretInput) + `serverUrl` + `username` |
| Env vars | `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME` |
| Outbound structure | IRC-style `base:` + `attachedResults:` within `createChatChannelPlugin` |
| Default DM policy | `pairing` |
| Production deps | None (fictional platform) |

### Two Things to Verify During Implementation
1. **`PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`** export location — likely `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing` or `channel-plugin-common`; check before writing `runtime-api.ts`
2. **`createChatChannelPlugin` outbound shape** — Nostr uses flat `outbound: { deliveryMode, textChunkLimit, sendText }` while IRC uses nested `outbound: { base: {...}, attachedResults: {...} }`; IRC's form is what the plan uses

### Verification After Implementation
```bash
pnpm test -- extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.test.ts
pnpm check
pnpm build
```

1	import {
2	  AllowFromListSchema,
3	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
4	  DmPolicySchema,
5	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
6	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";
7	import { buildSecretInputSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input";
8	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
9	
10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);
98

} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

Now I have enough information to start creating the PulseBoard extension. Let me plan what I need to understand:

1. `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE` is from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-plugin-common` (or re-exported from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`)
2. The IRC extension uses `outbound.base` and `outbound.attachedResults` while Nostr uses flat `outbound`
3. The plan says to use IRC-style (with `base:` + `attachedResults:`)

For PulseBoard:
- Group target format: `#board-name`


- DM target format: `username` (strips `@`)
- Chat types: `["direct", "group"]`
- Text chunk limit: 4000
- Auth: apiKey + serverUrl + username
- Env vars: `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`
- Default DM policy: `pairing`

Now I need to verify that `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE` is properly exported from the channel plugin common module, which traces back to the pairing message file.

I can see that the IRC runtime API re-exports this constant from the plugin SDK, so I should be able to import it directly from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-plugin-common` for the PulseBoard implementation. Setting up the runtime API exports for PulseBoard, I need to re-export the core types and constants from the SDK subpaths since there's no pre-built facade like the IRC or Nostr plugins have—pulling in `ChannelPlugin`, `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, and `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE` from the channel plugin common module, along with `OpenClawConfig` from the config runtime. Looking at what each extension does, I see the pattern now — they re-export from their own SDK entry points. For PulseBoard, I need to identify which generic SDK modules to expose, so I'm checking what `channel.ts` actually imports: `createChatChannelPlugin`, the constants and types from `channel-plugin-common`, and `OpenClawConfig` from `config-runtime`.

I'm also noticing the status helpers and extension-shared utilities that are needed, plus I should verify whether `collectStatusIssuesFromLastError` is available. Looking at how the Nostr implementation structures its `runtime-api.ts`, I can see it re-exports a specific set of utilities that `channel.ts` depends on — things like `buildChannelConfigSchema`, the status collectors, and the authorizer creators.

For PulseBoard's simpler case, I'll need a more minimal set since there's no real inbound DM handling — just the core types like `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `ChannelPlugin`, and `PluginRuntime`, plus any message constants that might be needed. Now I have enough to start building all the files. Let me understand the full plan:

1. `openclaw.plugin.json` - discovery manifest
2. `package.json` - @openclaw/pulseboard, no prod deps
3. `index.ts` - defineChannelPluginEntry
4. `api.ts` - export * from ./runtime-api.js
5. `setup-entry.ts` - defineSetupPluginEntry
6. `runtime-api.ts` - aggregated re-exports from generic SDK subpaths
7. `src/types.ts` - PulseBoardAccountConfig, ResolvedPulseBoardAccount


8. `src/config-schema.ts` - Zod schema for validation
9. `src/runtime.ts` - createPluginRuntimeStore to manage state
10. `src/accounts.ts` - single-account resolver with environment variable support
11. `src/normalize.ts` - logic to distinguish between board groups and direct messages
12. `src/send.ts` - message sending implementation
13. `src/monitor.ts` - inbound message monitoring with WebSocket handling
14. `src/setup-core.ts` - ChannelSetupAdapter for configuration
15. `src/setup-surface.ts` - Channel UI surface
16. `src/channel.ts` - the main plugin definition with createChatChannelPlugin
17. `src/channel.test.ts` - Vitest test suite

content.text:
All 17 files are in place. Here's a summary of what was built:

---

## PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Implementation Complete

All **17 new files** have been created under `extensions/pulseboard/`. Zero existing files were modified.

### Files Created

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Discovery manifest (`id: pulseboard`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`) |
| `package.json` | `@openclaw/pulseboard`, no production deps, full `openclaw` metadata block |
| `index.ts` | `defineChannelPluginEntry` wiring `pulseBoardPlugin` + `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `api.ts` | `export * from "./runtime-api.js"` |
| `setup-entry.ts` | `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` |
| `runtime-api.ts` | Thin re-export barrel from `channel-plugin-common`, `config-runtime`, `channel-config-primitives`, `status-helpers` |
| `src/types.ts` | `PulseBoardAccountConfig` and `ResolvedPulseBoardAccount` interfaces |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema with `apiKey`, `serverUrl`, `username`, `dmPolicy`, `allowFrom`, `boards` |
| `src/runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>` |
| `src/accounts.ts` | Single-account resolver with `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY/USERNAME/SERVER_URL` env fallbacks |
| `src/normalize.ts` | `#board-name` (group) vs `username` (DM) normalization, allow-entry normalization |
| `src/send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseBoard` stub (fictional platform, no real HTTP) |
| `src/monitor.ts` | `monitorPulseBoard` WebSocket stub that calls `onConnect/onDisconnect` hooks |
| `src/setup-core.ts` | `ChannelSetupAdapter` for CLI `--api-key/--username/--server-url` path |
| `src/setup-surface.ts` | `ChannelSetupWizard` with credential (`token`→apiKey), textInputs (`userId`→username, `relayUrls`→boards), dmPolicy, disable |
| `src/channel.ts` | `createChatChannelPlugin` — chatTypes `["direct","group"]`, 4000-char outbound, IRC-style `base`+`attachedResults`, pairing, DM security |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | 50+ Vitest tests covering meta, capabilities, config adapter, messaging normalization, outbound, pairing, security, and account helpers |

### Key design choices

- **Account model**: single top-level account at `channels.pulseboard.*` (same as Nostr)
- **Group target**: `#board-name` (normalized lowercase); **DM target**: `username` (strips leading `@`)
- **Text chunk limit**: **4000 characters**
- **Outbound**: IRC-style `base: { deliveryMode, textChunkLimit }` + `attachedResults: { channel, sendText }`
- **Default DM policy**: `pairing`
- **Auth**: `apiKey` (SecretInput) + `username` + optional `serverUrl`; env vars `PULSEBOARD_API_KEY`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`, `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`
